posts.title: Welcome to my corner of the web
posts.author: You
posts.excerpt: Why I built this site, and what you can expect to find here.
posts.body:
I wanted a quiet place on the internet that felt like *mine* — somewhere to share work, write down what I'm learning, and keep a record of where I've been.

## Why a personal site

Social platforms come and go, and they shape what you make. A site you own is different. It can be slow, considered, and strange in exactly the ways you want.

## What you'll find here

- **Projects** — things I've designed and built
- **Experience** — the road that got me here
- **Blog** — notes, essays, and the occasional rabbit hole

> Make the thing you wish existed.

Thanks for stopping by. More soon.

posts.title: Designing with restraint
posts.author: You
posts.excerpt: A few principles I keep coming back to when a design feels noisy.
posts.body:
Every project reaches a point where it feels *too busy*. Here's how I find my way back to calm.

## Remove before you add

The fastest way to improve most screens is to take something away. Whitespace is not wasted space — it's where the eye rests.

## Limit your palette

A small set of earthy, related tones will almost always feel more intentional than a rainbow. Constraints breed coherence.

## Let type do the work

Good typography carries a design further than any decoration. Get the rhythm right and you need very little else.

posts.title: The tools I reach for in 2026
posts.author: You
posts.excerpt: A short, honest list of the software that earns a place in my day.
posts.body:
I try to keep my toolkit small. These are the ones that have stuck around.

- **A plain text editor** for writing and code
- **A vector tool** for design work
- **A notebook** — the paper kind — for thinking

The best tool is usually the one that gets out of your way.
